q: What does ORB stand for?
explain: Opening Range Breakout — the first 30m candle of NY AM defines the range, and a break of either side triggers entry.

q: On NOVA's strategy, what is the Opening Range bar?
explain: The 9:30 ET 30-minute candle defines OR high + OR low. Triggers arm at 10:00.

q: When do NOVA's stop-orders arm?
explain: Triggers arm at 10:00 ET — long stop = OR_high+1tick, short stop = OR_low−1tick.

q: What's NOVA's hard session-flat time?
explain: 11:00 ET. If neither TP nor SL hits, position is closed flat at session end.

q: What is NOVA's risk per trade in dollars?
explain: $500 fixed stop loss. Strategy edge comes from the setup, not from sizing.

q: What is NOVA's take-profit in dollars?
explain: $2,000 fixed target. That's a 4R structural target on $500 risk.

q: How many NOVA trades fire per day, max?
explain: One trade per day, max. The first side of the OR to break wins; the other is canceled.

q: What's NOVA's daily loss cap?
explain: $500 daily loss cap. Pipeline halts if the cap is hit.

q: What ticker does NOVA trade?
explain: NOVA trades the E-mini Nasdaq (NQ) front-month contract.

q: What timeframe is NOVA built on?
explain: 30-minute. Picked specifically because a 30m candle close cleanly maps the OR.

q: On the NQ E-mini, what's $1 of P/L worth in points?
explain: 1 point on NQ E-mini = $20. So $1 = 0.05 pts. (Mini ≠ micro.)

q: What's NOVA's R-multiple on a winning trade?
Where does NOVA route the actual trade order?
explain: TradingView fires the alert, Railway validates and forwards, TradersPost executes on the prop firm.

q: What does PDH stand for?
explain: Prior Day High — yesterday's session high. A key reference level.

q: What does BOS stand for in market structure?
explain: Break Of Structure — price closing beyond a prior swing high/low confirms direction shift.

q: What does FVG stand for?
explain: Fair Value Gap — a 3-candle imbalance where price moves so fast it leaves an inefficiency.

q: Which is highest-impact for NQ at 8:30 ET?
explain: NFP. Major USD print = NQ vol spike. NOVA respects pre-cash-open prints by waiting for 9:30.

q: What's the NYSE cash open in ET?
explain: 9:30 AM ET. NOVA's strategy is built around cash-open liquidity.

q: Which session has the tightest NQ spreads?
explain: NY AM — peak liquidity = tightest spreads = cleanest fills. That's why NOVA only trades this window.

q: What does NOVA do if price wicks through the OR but doesn't close beyond it?
explain: Trigger immediately on the tick. Stop-entry orders fire on a single tick beyond OR_high or OR_low — first side wins.

q: On a NOVA winning trade, after how much profit does breakeven arm?
explain: BE arms at $500 in profit — SL slides to entry to lock the trade against loss.

q: After how much profit does the trail kick in?
explain: Trail engages at $750 in profit, ratcheting the protective stop higher.

q: Which prop firm is NOT in Sir's founder fleet?
explain: FTMO. Apex 100K, Lucid 50K, and Topstep are the founder fleet.

q: What's Sir's daily loss cap on a $50K Lucid eval?
explain: NOVA's own $500 daily loss cap is more conservative than the Lucid drawdown — the strategy itself is the firewall.

q: Why does NOVA use stop-entries, not close-confirmation?
explain: Stop-entries on the tick catch the impulsive leg of the breakout. Waiting for a candle close gives back R.

q: What's the NQ E-mini contract size?
explain: $20 per point on the mini. (Micro is $2 per point.)

q: What's NQ's tick size in points?
explain: 0.25 points per tick = $5 per tick on the E-mini.

q: What's the technical name for the 9:30-9:45 burst on NQ?
explain: Opening Drive (sometimes called Initial Drive) — the impulsive move right after the bell, often setting the day's tone.

q: Which day of the week historically has the best NY AM ORB win rate?
explain: Wednesday — typically post-trend day with cleaner directional moves. (Verify in the backtest.)

q: What does liquidity sweep mean?
options: Wick taking out a prior high/low then reversing
explain: A wick that pierces a prior swing point (often a stop cluster) then reverses — institutional capture before the real move.

q: The NOVA pipeline halts under which condition?
explain: All three trigger halts — daily loss cap, max trades per day, and the weekend session block.

q: Which best describes the ICT 'kill zone'?
explain: A specific time window with elevated institutional activity. NY AM 9:30-11:00 ET overlaps the NY kill zone.

q: Why does NOVA only trade NQ?
options: Tightest NY AM ORB statistical edge
explain: NQ has the cleanest NY AM ORB statistical edge across the futures complex — high vol, deep liquidity, clean breakouts.

q: What does the bot do if Railway state shows a 'ghost' open position past session end?
explain: Auto-clear — Railway's expire_stale_positions sweep clears state when the session window closes.

q: What's the canonical NOVA backtest WR (Jan 2025 - Apr 2026, 30m, 322 trades)?
explain: 82.6% — verified in the Pine Strategy Tester report.

q: What's the canonical NOVA backtest profit factor?
explain: 7.78 — gross profit divided by gross loss across 322 trades.

q: When did NOVA's first live forward-test fill execute?
explain: April 20, 2026 on Apex 100K + Lucid 50K founder accounts. Beta cohort onboarded April 27.

q: What's the maximum number of NOVA fires expected per WEEK?
explain: Up to 5 — one per trading day. Some days resolve no-trade (~30% of days).

q: What does the bot do when Sir hits the daily loss cap mid-session?
explain: Halt fanout immediately. No new entries; existing position runs to its bracket.

q: Why does NOVA skip Friday's last 30m of NY AM ORB?
explain: NOVA does NOT skip Friday — it trades all 5 weekdays equally. Trick question. The session is always 9:30-11:00 ET.
